question: Implement a C++ program to concatenate two C-style strings (character arrays) into a third C-style string without using standard library functions like `strcat`.
errors: The `my_strcat_inefficient` function exhibits poor efficiency. Specifically, within the loop that appends `src2` to `dest`, it repeatedly re-scans the `dest` string to find its current null terminator (`while (dest[current_dest_len] != '\0')`).

This repeated scanning makes the time complexity of the append operation approximately O(length(src2)^2) in the worst case, instead of the optimal O(length(src2)). The overall complexity becomes O(length(src1) + length(src2)^2) instead of O(length(src1) + length(src2)).
A more efficient approach would be to maintain a single pointer to the current end of `dest` and increment it as characters are copied, avoiding redundant scans.
